_note: SYNTHETIC DEMO FIXTURE — not investment advice. Scripted buy-side diligence call for the Diligence-Call Copilot demo, grounded in the topics Apple raised on its Q2 FY2026 earnings call (Apr 30 2026): a supply-gated iPhone 17 / Mac supercycle, the memory-cost gross-margin overhang plus the shift away from net-cash-neutral, and the forward call on Services / advertising / AI. Each researcher turn carries an `expected` block = the golden coverage verdict the engine should produce. Both the stage script and the test/driver fixture.
summary: Three questions. q1 (supply-gated hardware supercycle) ticks green cleanly. q2 (margin + capital) goes amber on an over-claimed 'margins are great, returning tons of cash' answer that glosses the building memory-cost overhang and the capital-allocation shift -> the copilot fires a grounded follow-up -> closes green with the real gross-margin bridge and the net-cash-neutral change. q3 (forward: Services / ads / AI) ticks green. Hang up all-green.

turns.speaker: analyst
turns.text: Thanks for the time. Let's start with the cycle. iPhone 17 looks like the strongest lineup you've ever had — where are you actually supply-constrained right now, iPhone versus Mac, and what's the root cause?

turns.speaker: researcher
turns.text: It's a genuine supercycle. iPhone revenue was up about twenty-two percent, the strongest cycle in our history, with a March-quarter record for upgraders and double-digit growth across most major markets. We're supply-constrained, not demand-constrained: iPhone was the binding constraint in the March quarter, and the constraint shifts to Mac — the mini, Studio, and MacBook Neo — into June. The root cause is availability of the advanced process nodes our SOCs are built on. MacBook Neo demand in particular has been off the charts.

extracted_facts: iPhone revenue up ~22%, strongest cycle in company history; March-quarter upgrader record
Supply-constrained, not demand-constrained: iPhone binding in the March quarter, shifting to Mac (mini, Studio, MacBook Neo) into June
Root cause is advanced-node SOC availability; MacBook Neo demand off the charts

turns.copilot_surfaced: q1 -> GREEN. Grounding: Q2 FY26 call — supply-gated supercycle, advanced-node SOC constraint shifting iPhone->Mac into June.

turns.speaker: analyst
turns.text: Good. On margins — where is gross margin running, and how should I think about it from here?

turns.speaker: researcher
turns.text: Margins are in great shape. Gross margin's been strong, mix is favorable, and we're returning a ton of cash to shareholders. Nothing I'd flag there — it's a really healthy picture.

extracted_facts: Characterizes gross margin as strong with favorable mix
Emphasizes capital return but gives no bridge and no caveat

contradiction.detail: 'Margins are great, nothing to flag' glosses the building memory-cost overhang the note models as a growing gross-margin headwind beyond June, and skips the capital-allocation shift — Apple dropping the net-cash-neutral target and lifting the buyback authorization by $100B. Either the headwind is being downplayed or the bridge is being skipped.

expected.followup: They glossed the bridge. Ask them to walk the gross-margin bridge — specifically where memory costs go beyond June and what offsets them (mix, tariffs, leverage, FX) — and to explain dropping net-cash-neutral and the $100B buyback increase.

turns.copilot_surfaced: q2 -> AMBER (partial). Glosses the memory-cost overhang + the net-cash-neutral change. Follow-up card fires. Hero moment.

turns.speaker: analyst
turns.prompted_by_copilot: Ask them to walk the gross-margin bridge — memory beyond June and the offsets — and explain dropping net-cash-neutral and the $100B buyback.
turns.text: Let me pin that down. Walk me through the gross-margin bridge — where do memory costs go beyond June and what offsets them — and separately, what does dropping the net-cash-neutral target and the hundred-billion buyback increase actually signal?

turns.speaker: researcher
turns.text: Fair. Company gross margin is around forty-nine percent. Memory is minimal-to-modest as a headwind right now, but it builds into a growing drag beyond the June quarter; this quarter we're helped by favorable mix and lower tariffs and hurt by some seasonal deleverage, with FX roughly neutral. On capital: we're moving away from net-cash-neutral as a formal target — we'll evaluate cash and debt independently — and we raised the buyback authorization by a hundred billion. So you're right to separate the strong headline from the memory trajectory.

extracted_facts: Memory minimal-to-modest now, building headwind beyond June; mix + lower tariffs help, seasonal deleverage hurts, FX ~neutral
Dropping net-cash-neutral as a formal target; buyback authorization raised by $100B
expected.thesis_delta: Margin bridge confirmed: ~49% GM with a memory-cost headwind building beyond June, partly offset by mix/tariffs. Capital-allocation posture shifted — net-cash-neutral target dropped, +$100B buyback. The initial 'nothing to flag' over-claim was corrected.

turns.copilot_surfaced: q2 -> GREEN. Bridge confirms the memory overhang beyond June + the capital shift. Thesis-delta card.

turns.speaker: analyst
turns.text: Last big one — the forward call. How durable are Services and the advertising lever, and what's the AI strategy and what's it costing you?

turns.speaker: researcher
turns.text: Services keeps compounding at high margin — services gross margin was up about twenty basis points sequentially on mix — and advertising is a fresh lever: App Store search ads are growing and we're launching Apple Maps ads in the US and Canada this summer. On AI, it's two-pronged — a collaboration with Google alongside our own in-house foundational models — and R&D is accelerating faster than overall opex to fund it. We're treating AI as incremental investment on top of the normal roadmap.

extracted_facts: Services compounding at high margin; services gross margin +~20bps sequentially on mix
Advertising lever: App Store search ads growing; Apple Maps ads launching US/Canada this summer
AI is two-pronged — Google collaboration plus in-house foundational models; R&D accelerating faster than opex

turns.copilot_surfaced: q3 -> GREEN. ALL 3 QUESTIONS GREEN. Zero holes.

turns.speaker: analyst
turns.text: That's my whole list. Appreciate you separating the headline margin from the memory trajectory — that was the one I most needed to nail down.

thesis_delta.summary: Thesis intact and sharpened. The one live wobble — management's 'margins are great, nothing to flag' over-claim — was corrected under follow-up to the real bridge (~49% GM with a memory-cost headwind building beyond June) and the capital-allocation shift (net-cash-neutral dropped, +$100B buyback). The supply-gated iPhone 17 / Mac supercycle and the Services / advertising / AI forward call both held.

changes.to: ~49% GM with a building memory-cost headwind beyond June, partly offset by mix/tariffs
target dropped; cash and debt evaluated independently; +$100B buyback authorization

thesis_delta.net: Hold / add. The load-bearing checks — supply-gated demand strength and the memory-cost margin trajectory — both held once the bridge was forced out.
